rulings.Case Title: RITA H SCHUMAN ET AL VS. TOWN AND COUNTRY INS. SER. INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 1. PLAINTIFFS RITA SCHUMAN, ANDREW SCHUMAN, and STEPHANIE SCHUMAN'S Notice Of Motion. The unopposed motion to restore this case to active status is granted. The stay is hereby lifted. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 391.1 In The Amount Of $75,000 And To Stay Proceedings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 2. 2-DEFENDANTS HEALTHRIGHT 360, DENISE WILLIAMS, and VITKA EISEN'S Motion Requiring Plaintiff To Furnish Security Pursuant To C.C.P. Section 391.1 In The Amount Of $75,000 And To Stay Proceedings. Defendants' (Healthright 360, Denise Williams, and Vitka Eisen) motion requiring plaintiff to furnish security pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 391.1 ("Sec. 391.1"), in the amount of $75,000 is GRANTED. The court orders plaintiff Raymond Vincent DiGiacomo, Jr. to furnish the security by June 8, 2026. The court issues an order to show cause re: dismissal and sets the OSC for hearing on June 22, 2026, at 9 a.m. in Department 301. Any party may file a brief of no more than five pages by June 15, 2026 concerning why the action should or should not be dismissed for failure to post security. The matter remains stayed until plaintiff posts security or until June 22, 2026. ***THE COURT'S COMPLETE TENATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO THE PARTIES*** For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: LI CHANG KUANG VS. KENNY WONG ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 3. DEFENDANT KENNY WONG'S MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS. The motion to quash service of summons is denied. Specially appearing defendant Kenny Wong contends that he was not properly personally served at the Hall of Justice on March 17, 2026. He challenges the filed proof of service of summons (Ex. A to the Wong Declaration) by providing a declaration that his name was spoken and papers were dropped; he also provides a declaration from a witness. Plaintiff provides a declaration from the process server attesting to hand-to-hand service. "When a defendant challenges the court's personal jurisdiction on the ground of improper service of process, the burden is on the plaintiff to prove the existence of jurisdiction by proving, inter alia, the facts requisite to an effective service." (Summers v. McClanahan (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 403, 413 [internal quotation marks, citation, and footnote omitted].) The declaration of a process server establishes a presumption affecting the burden of producing evidence that service was properly made. (Evid. Code, Sec. 647.) Wong has met his initial burden of producing evidence to overcome the filed proof of service. The court ultimately finds, however, that plaintiff has met his burden of showing that service was properly made. The court credits the declaration of registered process server Ian Martin because it is specific and descriptive, and because his status as a registered process server provides him with strong incentive to be truthful and accurate in declarations to the court. Even if the court credited Wong's declarations, it would nonetheless deny the motion. Actual notice may defeat a motion to quash when there is substantial compliance with the service statute. (Ramos v. Homeward Residential, Inc. (2014) 223 Cal.App.4th 1434, 1443-1444.) Here, Wong has actual notice and the court finds substantial compliance. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

Inc.S Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $1,935.00. The unopposed motion to compel defendant Conklin Bros., Inc., to respond to plaintiff's requests for production of documents, set one, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 2031.300, subd. (a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order. In addition, the court awards reasonable sanctions of $500 on this motion; Defendant shall pay this sanction to plaintiff within 30 days. (Id., subd. (c); Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) The court does not find substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. Plaintiff's motion to deem admitted the truth of any matters specified in his first set of requests for admission to defendant Conklin Bros. Inc. is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 2033.280, subd. (a).) The truth of any matters specified in the RFAs is deemed admitted by this defendant. (Id., subds. (b), (c).) In addition, the court awards reasonable sanctions of $500 on this motion; Defendant shall pay this sanction to plaintiff within 30 days. (Id., subd. (c); Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) Sanctions are mandatory on this motion. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Conklin Bros. Inc.S Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of 1,935.00; And Declaration Of Jonathan M. Kashani, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 6. 1-PLAINTIFF CURTIS ADDISON's Motion To Compel Defendant Conklin Bros. Inc.'s Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of 1,935.00. The unopposed motion to compel defendant Conklin Bros., Inc., to respond to plaintiff's form interrogatories, set one, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 2030.290, subd. (a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order. In addition, the court awards reasonable sanctions of $500 on this motion; Defendant shall pay this sanction to plaintiff within 30 days. (Id., subd. (c); Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) The court does not find substantial justification. Plaintiff asks for the sanction to be levied jointly against defendant's counsel; the court may not do so without making a finding that counsel advised the conduct and has no basis to make such a finding. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas Smith Richardss Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $1,935.00; And Declaration Of Jonathan M. Kashani, Esq.
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 6. 8-PLAINTIFF CURTIS ADDISON's Motion To Compel Defendant Thomas Smith Richardss Responses To Special Interrogatories, Set One; Request For Monetary Sanctions And Costs Against Defendant And Defendants Counsel Of Record, Jointly And Severally, In The Amount Of $1,935.00. The unopposed motion to compel defendant Thomas Smith Richards to respond to plaintiff's special interrogatories, set one, is granted. Defendant failed to timely respond to this discovery and has thereby waived objections. (Code Civ. Proc., Sec. 2030.290, subd. (a).) Defendant is ordered to serve verified responses, without objections, within 10 court days of entry of this order. In addition, the court awards reasonable sanctions of $500 on this motion; Defendant shall pay this sanction to plaintiff within 30 days. (Id., subd. (c); Rule of Court 3.1348, subd. (a).) The court does not find substantial justification. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: FEDERICO MELENDEZ-DOMINGUEZ VS. MOBILITAS INSURANCE COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Petition And Petition To Compel Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the LAW AND MOTION / DISCOVERY Calendar for Thursday, May-07-2026, LINE 7. PLAINTIFF FEDERICO MELENDEZ-DOMINGUEZ', AN INDIVIDUAL, Petition To Compel Arbitration. Petitioner's petition to compel arbitration is continued to July 15, 2026. Petitioner and respondent (Mobilitas Insurance Company) agree that this underinsured motorist bodily injury claim is subject to arbitration. (Salazar Decl., par. 12, Ex. C; Zhou Decl., par. 10, Ex. 1; Insurance Code Sec. 11580.2(f).) Notwithstanding that agreement and almost six months after petitioner's formal demand, the arbitration process has yet to commence. The parties are ordered to meet and confer regarding (1) the selection of an arbitrator; (2) exchanging discovery; and (3) coordinating an arbitration schedule. On or before July 1, 2026, each party may file a brief up to seven pages that identifies the parties' progress and any outstanding disputes. The court anticipates that the parties will resolve these issues and take the continued hearing off calendar. If the parties have not agreed to an arbitrator by the continued hearing date, they are ordered to provide three names each of acceptable arbitrators and the court will make a selection. The motion is moot as to respondent Lyft, Inc. Plaintiff dismissed Lyft, Inc. on April 27, 2026. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/CVA)
